_comment: Month 1 guided recipes (Menu A + B), authored verbatim from docs/T2D_Beginner_Edition.md. Self-contained Coach content with steps inline — NOT in the read-only recipes.json. Doneness verdicts use the cited USDA rules in doneness.json (e.g. whole cuts stay 145°F even where the plan suggests med-rare).

recipes.title: Sausage & Spinach Egg Bites
recipes.note: Makes 12. Fridge 4 days; freeze the rest. Reheat 30–45 sec. Eat 3 per person.

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Grease all 12 muffin cups well, or use silicone liners — egg bites cement themselves to bare metal.
In a skillet over medium-high, cook 8 oz sausage, breaking it up, until no pink remains (~6 min). Drain and let cool a few minutes.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep-browned crumbles, glistening rendered fat, no raw red.

steps.text: Whisk 10 eggs with ¼ cup cream and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Stir in the cooked sausage, 5 oz chopped spinach, and 1 cup shredded cheddar. Pour evenly into the cups, about ¾ full.
Bake 18–20 min until the centers are set with no liquid jiggle. Cool 5 min, then lift out.
steps.sensory_cue: Tops puffed and matte and springy; the center has at most a faint wobble (firm jello, not liquid).

steps.text: Cook the chicken first (great for Sunday): preheat the oven to 400°F. Rub a chicken breast with oil, salt, and pepper. Roast on a sheet pan 22–25 min until 165°F, then rest 5 min and slice.
steps.sensory_cue: White all the way through with clear juices.

steps.text: Pour 2 tbsp dressing into the bottom of each jar.
Add 4 oz sliced chicken, 1 chopped hard-boiled egg, 1 crumbled bacon strip, 2 tbsp blue cheese, and ¼ cup halved tomatoes.
Pack romaine on top to keep everything dry. Seal and refrigerate up to 4 days.
Morning-of: add ½ diced avocado. At lunch, shake to coat.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Cut 1.5 lb broccoli into florets. Toss with 2 tbsp olive oil, ½ tsp garlic powder, and salt. Spread on a sheet pan in one layer.
Rub chicken thighs with oil and salt and nestle them among the broccoli.
Roast 20–25 min until the broccoli edges brown and the chicken reads 165°F.
steps.sensory_cue: Broccoli edges blistered; chicken white throughout with clear juices.

recipes.title: Beef & Pepper Stir-Fry
recipes.note: Thin stir-fry strips cook too fast to thermometer reliably — go by browning. If you check the pan beef, 145°F is the safe whole-cut minimum.

steps.text: Slice 1 lb steak thinly against the grain and pat dry. Slice 2 bell peppers and ½ onion.
steps.sensory_cue: Cut across the meat's fibers, not along them — that's what makes a slice tender instead of chewy.

steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over high until it shimmers.
Add the beef in one layer; don't stir for 1 min, then sear 2–3 min until browned. Remove.
steps.sensory_cue: Edges turn from red to deep brown; it releases from the pan when it's ready.

steps.text: Add the peppers and onion; stir-fry 4–5 min until crisp-tender.
Return the beef; add 2 tbsp soy sauce and 1 minced garlic clove; toss 1 min.
Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp: soak frozen shrimp in cold water 15 min, then drain and pat very dry. Make zoodles from 3 zucchini with a spiralizer or peeler ribbons.
steps.sensory_cue: Dry shrimp sears; wet shrimp steams and turns rubbery — pat them really dry.

steps.text: Melt 3 tbsp butter in a skillet over medium. Add 3 minced garlic cloves and cook 30 sec — don't let them brown.
Add the shrimp in one layer; cook about 2 min per side until pink and curled into a loose C. Remove.
steps.sensory_cue: Pink/coral and opaque, curled into a loose C. A tight O means overcooked — the window is about 30 seconds, so hover.

steps.text: Add the zoodles; toss 2 min just to warm — any longer makes them watery.
Return the shrimp; finish with a squeeze of lemon and parsley.
Make cauliflower mash from 1 head: boil florets ~10 min until fork-tender, drain, and press out the water before blending with butter, cream, and salt.
steps.sensory_cue: Press in the colander until cloudy water stops streaming and the surface looks dry — skipping the drain makes soup.

steps.text: Pat 2 pork chops dry and season both sides.
Heat 1 tbsp oil over medium-high. Sear the chops about 4–5 min per side until 145°F.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep golden crust; the center reaches 145°F.

steps.text: Rest 3 min, then serve over the mash.
Form 1 lb ground beef into 2 patties and season.
Heat a skillet over medium-high. Sear the patties 3–4 min per side until 160°F.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink in the center; juices run clear.

steps.text: Make a bed of chopped romaine in 2 bowls.
Top each with a patty, ¼ cup cheddar (it melts on the hot patty), tomatoes, and sugar-free ketchup/mustard.

recipes.title: Vanilla Chia Pudding + Berries
recipes.note: No cooking. Savory swap: ham & cheese egg muffins using the egg-bite method.

steps.text: In a bowl, whisk 6 tbsp chia seeds, 1.5 cups almond milk, ½ tsp vanilla, and sweetener to taste.
Wait 5 min, then whisk again to break up any clumps.
Divide into 5 jars and chill overnight — it sets to a pudding.
Each morning, top one jar with ¼ cup berries.
Sunday: thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp, then sauté in 1 tbsp oil over medium-high about 2 min per side until pink and curled. Chill.
steps.sensory_cue: Pink, opaque, curled into a loose C.

steps.text: Build each box: 5 oz shrimp, romaine, ¼ cup tomatoes, 2 tbsp dressing on the side, and a lime wedge.
Add ½ avocado morning-of.
Season 1 lb chicken with fajita seasoning; sear over medium-high 5–6 min per side until 165°F, then slice. (Skip if your chicken is pre-cooked.)
Slice 2 peppers and ½ onion; sauté in 1 tbsp oil over medium-high 6 min until soft and charred at the edges.
In another pan, sauté 2 bags cauliflower rice in 1 tbsp oil 5 min.
Build bowls: cauliflower rice, chicken, peppers and onion, topped with cheese.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Mix 1.25 lb ground meat with 1 egg, ¼ cup mozzarella, and salt/pepper. Roll about 16 balls.
steps.sensory_cue: The mix should be tacky and hold together when pinched — too wet? add a spoon of almond flour or parmesan.

steps.text: Bake on a sheet pan 18 min until 160°F.
steps.sensory_cue: No pink at the center of a split meatball.

steps.text: Warm marinara in a pot; add the meatballs and simmer 5 min.
Make zoodles from 3 zucchini; toss in a hot dry pan 2 min. Serve the sauce and meatballs over the zoodles.
Thaw and dry 1 lb shrimp. Trim 1.5 lb green beans.
Sauté the green beans in 3 tbsp butter over medium-high 6 min until crisp-tender.
Push the beans aside; add the shrimp and 3 minced garlic cloves; cook about 2 min per side until pink and curled.
steps.sensory_cue: Loose C, pink and opaque throughout.

recipes.title: Steak & Roasted Asparagus
recipes.note: The plan suggests medium-rare (130–135°F); 145°F is the USDA safe minimum the coach checks against.

steps.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Toss 1.5 lb asparagus with oil and salt; roast 12 min.
Pat 1 lb steak dry and season. Heat a skillet over high; sear 3–4 min per side.
steps.sensory_cue: Deep mahogany crust. For safety, cook to 145°F; a 3-minute rest follows.

steps.text: Rest 5 min, then slice against the grain.
steps.sensory_cue: Slice perpendicular to the visible fibers so each piece is tender, not stringy.

steps.text: Slice 14 oz smoked sausage into coins. Brown in a skillet over medium-high 5 min (it's already cooked — you're just browning).
Add ½ shredded cabbage, ½ sliced onion, and 2 tbsp butter.
Sauté 10 min until the cabbage is tender.
